_note: Seed hazard dataset. Entries are factual mechanism-level notes, not policy. Extend at <project>/.tool/safety/local_hazards.json (same schema) to overlay project-specific entries.

entries.mechanism: Fluoride penetrates intact skin and chelates serum calcium, producing systemic hypocalcemia and arrhythmia after delayed pain onset. Burns continue to deepen for hours after exposure.
entries.first_aid: Copious water flush, then calcium gluconate gel massaged into the burn. Seek emergency care for any HF exposure regardless of size.

entries.mechanism: Inhibits mitochondrial cytochrome c oxidase. Olfactory paralysis above ~100 ppm removes the smell warning before the lethal exposure level.
entries.first_aid: Immediate evacuation to fresh air. Rescue requires SCBA; unprotected rescuers regularly become second victims.
ppe: SCBA for entry above action level
entries.severity: rapid loss of consciousness above ~500 ppm

entries.mechanism: Binds hemoglobin ~240x more avidly than O2, blocking oxygen transport. Symptoms (headache, confusion) mimic flu and are easy to dismiss until collapse.
entries.first_aid: Move to fresh air; high-flow O2; 100% normobaric or hyperbaric depending on severity and pregnancy status.
ppe: CO alarm in any space with combustion
do not run engines in enclosed spaces
entries.severity: lethal at sustained exposures above ~1500 ppm

entries.mechanism: Bleach + ammonia releases chloramine gas; bleach + acid (vinegar, muriatic) releases chlorine gas. Both irritate airways and at high concentration cause chemical pneumonitis.
entries.first_aid: Evacuate, ventilate, fresh air. Persistent cough or chest tightness gets medical evaluation.
ppe: never combine bleach with other cleaners
entries.severity: low concentration is irritant; enclosed space is acutely hazardous

entries.mechanism: Internal short or physical damage initiates exothermic decomposition; vented gases (CO, HF, H2) are flammable and the fire is self-sustaining once propagation begins between cells.
entries.first_aid: Evacuate, isolate the cell in noncombustible container, copious water to cool surrounding cells. Class D extinguisher does not help; water mass does.
ppe: isolate cells in sand or steel container while working
entries.severity: small cells release significant heat and toxic gas

entries.mechanism: Volatile at room temperature; vapor crosses the alveolar membrane and accumulates in the CNS, kidneys. Carpet and porous floors trap spills indefinitely.
entries.first_aid: Ventilate the room, evacuate while a spill kit (sulfur or zinc amalgam powder, mercury sponges) is brought in. Do not vacuum: standard vacuums aerosolize Hg.
ppe: respirator with mercury cartridge for cleanup of beads
entries.severity: chronic exposure produces irreversible neurologic damage
